context.profile.md:
Name: Jane Smith
Email: jane@example.com
Skills: Python, SQL, team leadership
Work Experience:
- Senior Analyst at DataCo (Jan 2020 – Present): Built ETL pipelines, reduced query time 40%
- Analyst at StartupX (Mar 2018 – Dec 2019): Delivered dashboards for 5 product teams
context.narrative.md:
preferred_template: tech
Positioning: Data engineer who bridges analytics and engineering
context.jd.md:
Role: Senior Data Engineer
Must-Have Skills: Python, SQL, stakeholder management
Keywords for ATS: ETL pipelines, data modeling, stakeholder management, Python, SQL, CI/CD

tests.expected_output: A tailored resume appended to materials.md using the tech template, with ATS Keyword Analysis block showing coverage count and any missing keywords listed with suggested placements.
assertions: materials.md is created or appended with a resume section
Resume uses 'Technical Skills' section heading (tech template)
ATS Keyword Analysis block appears with 'Coverage: X of Y keywords present'
Output tells user to run /export-resume and /write-cover-letter
narrative.md preferred_template remains 'tech' (already set)
No em-dashes in bullet text
Dates use 'Mon YYYY' format with en-dash separator

context.profile.md:
Name: John Doe
Email: john@example.com
Work Experience:
- Head of Analytics at FinCo (Jun 2019 – Present): Led 12-person team, $2M budget, 35% revenue growth
- Director, Data at BankCo (Jan 2016 – May 2019): Built enterprise BI platform

context.jd.md:
Role: VP of Analytics
Must-Have Skills: leadership, stakeholder management, P&L ownership
Keywords for ATS: team leadership, P&L ownership, stakeholder management, executive reporting, data strategy

tests.expected_output: A resume using the classic template structure with Work Experience section (not 'Professional Experience'), keyword gap analysis included.
assertions: Resume section heading is 'Work Experience' (approved heading, classic template)
ATS Keyword Analysis block present
preferred_template set to 'classic' in narrative.md
materials.md appended under bigbank/vp-analytics/
Summary section present and references VP-level scope

context.profile.md:
Name: Sara Lee
Email: sara@example.com
Work Experience:

- APM at StartupY (Jan 2019 – Feb 2021): Managed roadmap for B2B SaaS
context.jd.md:

Must-Have Skills: roadmap planning, cross-functional leadership
Keywords for ATS: roadmap planning, OKRs, cross-functional leadership, go-to-market, stakeholder alignment

tests.expected_output: Skill asks the user to choose a template since preferred_template is not set in narrative.md, then proceeds once user selects one. Saves selection to narrative.md.
assertions: Skill prompts user to choose: classic, modern, executive, or tech
After selection, preferred_template is written to narrative.md
Resume is generated and appended to materials.md
ATS compliance checklist shows all items checked

context.profile.md:

tests.expected_output: Template argument 'executive' overrides preferred_template 'modern' from narrative.md. Resume uses executive template structure with Scope line and Core Competencies section.

Resume contains 'Core Competencies' section
Each role has a 'Scope:' line with team/budget context
ATS Keyword Analysis block present

context.profile.md:
Name: Jane Smith
Email: jane@example.com
Phone: 555-0100
Work Experience:
- Senior Analyst at DataCo: Built ETL pipelines, reduced query time 40%
context.narrative.md: Positioning: Data engineer who bridges analytics and engineering. Target: fast-growing B2B SaaS companies.
context.research.md: Acme Corp mission: Make data accessible to every business. Recent news: Launched AcmeData platform Q1 2026.
context.jd.md: Role: Senior Data Engineer. Top requirements: ETL expertise, Python, cross-team collaboration.
context.team.md: Hiring Manager: David Park, Director of Engineering

tests.expected_output: A cover letter under 400 words addressed to David Park, referencing AcmeData platform launch, quantified accomplishment from profile, narrative thread, and a call to discuss.
assertions: Letter is addressed to 'David Park' (from team.md)
Mentions AcmeData platform or Acme Corp mission (from research.md)
Includes at least one quantified accomplishment
Total word count is 400 or fewer
No filler phrases: 'I am passionate', 'great fit', 'excited to'
Opening sentence does not begin with 'I am writing to apply'
Appended to materials.md under acme-corp/senior-engineer/

context.profile.md:

Work Experience:

context.jd.md: Role: VP of Analytics. Requirements: leadership, financial services experience, executive reporting.

Narrative thread connects analyst background to VP-level ambition

Signed with John Doe's name, email, and phone

Data engineer with 6 years experience.

### Senior Analyst — DataCo
*Jan 2020 – Present*
- Built ETL pipelines reducing query time 40%

---
## ATS Keyword Analysis
Coverage: 5 of 6 keywords present

tests.expected_output: Pandoc not available; skill prints install instructions and does not create resume.docx.
assertions: Skill detects pandoc is missing
Prints install instructions for macOS (brew install pandoc), Windows (winget install pandoc), and Linux (sudo apt install pandoc)
Does not create resume.docx
Does not leave a resume-temp.md file behind

- Built ETL pipelines reducing query time 40%

tests.expected_output: A valid JSON Resume v1.0.0 file written to resume.json with basics, work, education, and skills sections populated.
assertions: resume.json is created at the correct path
JSON is valid (parseable)
basics.name equals 'Jane Smith'
basics.email equals 'jane@example.com'
work array has at least one entry with name, position, startDate, and highlights
education array has at least one entry
skills array is present
User told to use resume-cli

context.materials.md:
---
# Resume — Senior Engineer at Acme Corp
# Template: tech
# Generated: 2026-06-27
---

Jane Smith

## Summary
Data engineer.

tests.expected_output: When no PDF engine (pdflatex, wkhtmltopdf, weasyprint) is installed, prints fallback message directing user to convert resume.docx in Word.
assertions: Checks for pdflatex, wkhtmltopdf, and weasyprint
When none found, prints fallback options
Mentions 'File → Export as PDF' Word option
Mentions wkhtmltopdf install command as an alternative
Does not create resume.pdf

jane@example.com

tests.expected_output: When pandoc IS available, uses modern-reference.docx and creates resume.docx at the correct path. Temp file is cleaned up after export.
assertions: Reads preferred_template 'modern' from narrative.md
Pandoc command uses modern-reference.docx
resume.docx is created at job-search/companies/acme-corp/positions/senior-engineer/resume.docx
resume-temp.md is deleted after successful conversion
User told to open in Word to review formatting
